reasoning:
Liu Zige's 200m butterfly world record (2:01.81) is one of the most durable 'supersuit' records, having stood since 2009. While Canadian prodigy Summer McIntosh came exceptionally close (2:01.99) at the World Aquatics Championships in Singapore in July 2025, the record survived that peak event. Wikipedia records as of November 4, 2025 (five days before the cutoff) continue to list Liu as the holder.

For the first resolution date (Nov 16, 2025), there are no major long-course (LC) international meets scheduled, and the short-course (SC) season is typically in effect. For the second date (Dec 9, 2025), the US Open (Dec 3-6) is a potential long-course threat, but 'legacy' world records are rarely broken at non-taper meets in December. For the third date (Feb 7, 2026), while the Pro Swim Series and Australian domestic meets begin, the statistical likelihood of breaking this specific record—which has resisted all challengers for 16 years—remains low outside of a primary summer championship. Uncertainty increases with each date as McIntosh and others remain within striking distance.
